pattern_recognition: Dynamic Programming - The problem exhibits optimal substructure and overlapping subproblems, as the minimum path sum to a cell depends on the minimum path sums to its adjacent cells.

approaches.title: Brute Force
approaches.intuition: Explore all possible paths from the top-left to the bottom-right corner, calculating the sum for each path.
approaches.algorithm: Use recursion to explore all paths and keep track of the minimum sum encountered.

approaches.title: Optimal Approach
approaches.intuition: Utilize dynamic programming to build a solution iteratively, storing the minimum path sums in a 2D array.
approaches.algorithm: Initialize a DP table, fill it based on the minimum sums from the top and left cells, and return the value in the bottom-right cell.

dry_run: Consider a grid: [[1,3,1],[1,5,1],[4,2,1]]. Start at (0,0) with sum 1. Move to (0,1) with sum 4, then to (0,2) with sum 5. Move down to (1,2) with sum 6, then down to (2,2) with sum 7. Alternatively, move down to (1,0) with sum 2, then to (1,1) with sum 7, and finally to (2,2) with sum 8. The minimum path sum is 7.
edge_cases: Empty grid, single cell grid, grid with all zeros, grid with large dimensions (e.g., 1000x1000), and grids that may cause integer overflow.
common_pitfalls: 1) Not considering paths that lead to dead ends; correct approach should track valid paths only. 2) Forgetting to initialize the DP table properly; ensure all base cases are handled. 3) Miscalculating the minimum sum by not considering both directions (down and right) correctly.
